vulnerability.title: Disable Compression Or Set Compression to delayed
vulnerability.description: Compression is useful for slow network connections over long distances but can cause performance issues on local LANs. If use of compression is required, it should be enabled only after a user has authenticated; otherwise, it should be disabled. To disable compression or delay compression until after a user has successfully authenticated, add or correct the following line in the /etc/ssh/sshd_config file: Compression
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then var_sshd_disable_compression='' mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" cp "/

triage_decision.reason: The recommendation disables SSH compression until after user authentication to prevent potential performance issues on local LANs and align with security hardening best practices.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; SSH service misconfiguration; Performance degradation on local LAN

execution_details.stdout:
#	$OpenBSD: sshd_config,v 1.104 2021/07/02 05:11:21 dtucker Exp $

# This is the sshd server system-wide configuration file.  See
# sshd_config(5) for more information.

# This sshd was compiled with PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/sbin

# The strategy used for options in the default sshd_config shipped with
# OpenSSH is to specify options with their default value where
# possible, but leave them commented.  Uncommented options override the
# default value.

# To modify the system-wide sshd configuration, create a  *.conf  file under
#  /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/  which will be automatically included below
Include /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf

# If you want to change the port on a SELinux system, you have to tell
# SELinux about this change.
# semanage port -a -t ssh_port_t -p tcp #PORTNUMBER
#
#Port 22
#AddressFamily any
#ListenAddress 0.0.0.0
#ListenAddress ::

#HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_rsa_key
#HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ecdsa_key
#HostKey /etc/ssh/ssh_host_ed25519_key

# Ciphers and keying
#RekeyLimit default none

# Logging
#SyslogFacility AUTH
#LogLevel INFO

# Authentication:

#LoginGraceTime 2m
#PermitRootLogin prohibit-password
#StrictModes yes
#MaxAuthTries 6
#MaxSessions 10

#PubkeyAuthentication yes

# The default is to check both .ssh/authorized_keys and .ssh/authorized_keys2
# but this is overridden so installations will only check .ssh/authorized_keys
AuthorizedKeysFile	.ssh/authorized_keys

#AuthorizedPrincipalsFile none

#AuthorizedKeysCommand none
#AuthorizedKeysCommandUser nobody

# For this to work you will also need host keys in /etc/ssh/ssh_known_hosts
#HostbasedAuthentication no
# Change to yes if you don't trust ~/.ssh/known_hosts for
# HostbasedAuthentication
#IgnoreUserKnownHosts no
# Don't read the user's ~/.rhosts and ~/.shosts files
#IgnoreRhosts yes

# To disable tunneled clear text passwords, change to no here!
#PasswordAuthentication yes
#PermitEmptyPasswords no

# Change to no to disable s/key passwords
#KbdInteractiveAuthentication yes

# Kerberos options
#KerberosAuthentication no
#KerberosOrLocalPasswd yes
#KerberosTicketCleanup yes
#KerberosGetAFSToken no
#KerberosUseKuserok yes

# GSSAPI options
#GSSAPIAuthentication no
#GSSAPICleanupCredentials yes
#GSSAPIStrictAcceptorCheck yes
#GSSAPIKeyExchange no
#GSSAPIEnablek5users no

# Set this to 'yes' to enable PAM authentication, account processing,
# and session processing. If this is enabled, PAM authentication will
# be allowed through the KbdInteractiveAuthentication and
# PasswordAuthentication.  Depending on your PAM configuration,
# PAM authentication via KbdInteractiveAuthentication may bypass
# the setting of "PermitRootLogin without-password".
# If you just want the PAM account and session checks to run without
# PAM authentication, then enable this but set PasswordAuthentication
# and KbdInteractiveAuthentication to 'no'.
# WARNING: 'UsePAM no' is not supported in RHEL and may cause several
# problems.
#UsePAM no

#AllowAgentForwarding yes
#AllowTcpForwarding yes
#GatewayPorts no
#X11Forwarding no
#X11DisplayOffset 10
#X11UseLocalhost yes
#PermitTTY yes
#PrintMotd yes
#PrintLastLog yes
#TCPKeepAlive yes
#PermitUserEnvironment no
#Compression delayed
#ClientAliveInterval 0
#ClientAliveCountMax 3
#UseDNS no
#PidFile /var/run/sshd.pid
#MaxStartups 10:30:100
#PermitTunnel no
#ChrootDirectory none
#VersionAddendum none

# no default banner path
#Banner none

# override default of no subsystems
Subsystem	sftp	/usr/libexec/openssh/sftp-server

# Example of overriding settings on a per-user basis
#Match User anoncvs
#	X11Forwarding no
#	AllowTcpForwarding no
#	PermitTTY no
#	ForceCommand cvs server

reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We are given a finding: "Disable Compression Or Set Compression to delayed" with Rule ID: xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sshd_disable_compression
 The goal is to disable compression (or set to delayed) in the SSH daemon configuration until after user authentication.

 According to the triage context and description:
   Compression is useful for slow network connections over long distances but can cause performance issues on local LANs.
   If use of compression is required, it should be enabled only after a user has authenticated; otherwise, it should be disabled.

 Therefore, to disable compression until after a user has successfully authenticated, we must set the Compression option in /etc/ssh/sshd_config to 'delayed'.

 Steps:
  1. Check if the line "Compression delayed" exists in /etc/ssh/sshd_config (note: the exact format might be "Compression delayed").
  2. If it exists, we are done.
  3. If it does not exist, we need to add or correct the line to be "Compression delayed".

 However, note the remediation script in the finding:
   - It suggests to add or correct the line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf? 
     But wait, the finding says: "add or correct the following line in the /etc/ssh/sshd_config file"

   Actually, the remediation script says:
      touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf
      chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf
      LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" ... 
      and then another sed for /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d

   This seems to be a mistake because the rule is for sshd_config, not sshd_config.d.

   Let me re-read the remediation script:

      "Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then var_sshd_disable_compression='' mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complian"

   This is messy. However, the rule ID is for sshd_disable_compression, which should be in the main sshd_config.

   The description says: "add or correct the following line in the /etc/ssh/sshd_config file"

   So we should focus on /etc/ssh/sshd_config.

   The remediation script might be incorrectly written, but the intent is clear: set Compression to delayed in sshd_config.

   Therefore, we ignore the remediation script's mention of sshd_config.d and focus on the main file.

   We are to set: Compression delayed

   How the line should look: exactly "Compression delayed"

   Note: The sshd_config file uses key value pairs, and the key is case-insensitive? But the value is case-sensitive? 
   However, the rule is checking for the string "Compression delayed".

   We'll use sed to either:
      - If the line starting with optional whitespace, then "Compression", then optional whitespace, then a '+' or more, then "Id", then optional whitespace, and then the rest of the line (which we don't care about) is present, replace the entire line with "Compression delayed"
      - If not found, insert "Compression delayed" at the end of the file.

   However, note: the rule might be case-insensitive? But the finding ID uses the exact string.

   Since the problem does not specify case-sensitivity, and the remediation script uses the exact string in the sed command, we assume exact match.

   But note: the value in sshd_config for Compression is typically one of: yes, no, delayed, etc. and they are case-insensitive? 
   Actually, the OpenSSH documentation says the values are case-insensitive.

   However, the rule is defined with the exact string "Compression delayed", so we must match that exactly.

   But to be safe, we can do a case-insensitive check? The problem doesn't specify.

   Given the context of the problem (security hardening) and the fact that the rule is defined with that exact string, we'll assume the system expects the exact string.

   However, the remediation script in the finding uses:
        sed -i "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config"

   This is case-sensitive for the pattern? The pattern uses \s* and \+ which are regex, and the letters are as given.

   We'll follow the remediation script's approach: use a regex that allows optional whitespace around the words and the plus.

   But note: the value we want to set is exactly "Compression delayed" (without any extra whitespace in the middle? Actually, the pattern allows optional whitespace and a plus, so we are matching lines that have "Compression", then optional whitespace, then one or more '+', then "Id", then optional whitespace.

   However, the line we want to put is "Compression delayed", which has a single space.

   So we can do:

      If we find a line matching: /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]*\+[[:space:]]*Id[[:space:]]/ 
          then replace that line with "Compression delayed"

      Else, append "Compression delayed"

   But note: the pattern in the remediation script uses \s+ for the space between Compression and the plus, and then \s* for the space after the plus and before Id? Actually, the pattern is: "/^\s*Compression\s\+/Id"

   This breaks down as:
        ^\s*           : optional whitespace at start
        Compression      : the literal string
        \s\+             : one or more whitespace
        Id               : the literal string

   So it matches lines that have (with optional leading space): 
        Compression, then one or more spaces, then the string "Id"

   Example: "Compression   Id" -> matches? 
        But note: the pattern requires a plus (one or more whitespace) between Compression and Id.

   So "Compression Id" (with a space) would match because \s+ matches one or more spaces.

   However, we want to set the value to "Compression delayed", which has a space between Compression and delayed.

   But the pattern we are matching on does not have the word "delayed", it has "Id". 

   This is confusing.

   Let me look at the actual sshd_config: the Compression option is set as:

        Compression yes
        Compression no
        Compression delayed

   So the line we are looking for is: "Compression delayed"

   The remediation script's pattern is trying to match lines that have the word "Compression" and then later the word "Id" (with whitespace in between). 

   This does not match our target line.

   Therefore, I suspect the remediation script in the finding is incorrect.

   Given the rule ID and the description, we are to set Compression to delayed in the main sshd_config.

   We'll do:

      If there is a line that starts (after optional whitespace) with "Compression", then optional whitespace, then the value (which we don't care about) and we want to change it to "delayed", but note the rule is about the exact string.

   However, the finding says: "Disable Compression Or Set Compression to delayed"

   And the remediation script in the finding is provided but seems to have a typo.

   Since we are to follow the instructions: use the tools to apply the fix, and the fixing commands are specified in the remediation script, but we see an error, we must rely on the rule description.

   We are told: "add or correct the following line in the /etc/ssh/sshd_config file: Compression"

   And the value should be set to delayed.

   So we do:

      If the file /etc/ssh/sshd_config contains a line that matches (case-insensitive) the pattern: 
          ^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]*.* 
          then we replace the value after the Compression keyword to be "delayed"

      But note: the entire line might be "Compression yes", we want to change to "Compression delayed"

      However, the rule might be expecting the exact string "Compression delayed" as the line.

   Given the confusion, and to be safe, we'll do two things:

      1. Check if the exact line "Compression delayed" exists (with exactly one space between Compression and delayed, and no extra whitespace at the beginning or end? but we allow optional whitespace at the beginning and end?).

      2. If not, then we check for any line that starts with optional whitespace, then "Compression", then optional whitespace and then any value (we don't care what the value is) and we change that line to have the value "delayed".

      3. If we didn't find any such line, we append "Compression delayed".

   However, the problem says: "You MUST use the provided tools: run_cmd, read_file, write_file."

   And we are to apply the fix.

   We'll design:

      Step 1: Read the file /etc/ssh/sshd_config.
      Step 2: Check if there is a line that matches (with optional leading and trailing whitespace) the string "Compression delayed" (exactly, with one space in the middle).
          If found, we are done.
      Step 3: If not found, then look for any line that matches (with optional leading whitespace) the pattern: 
          ^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+ 
          (meaning: starts with optional space, then the word Compression, then one or more spaces) 
          and then we replace that line with: 
                [the part up to and including the Compression and the spaces] + "delayed"
          But note: we want to preserve any trailing comments? The problem doesn't specify.

          However, the remediation script in the finding does not preserve comments. It replaces the whole line.

      Step 4: If we didn't find any line starting with Compression (with optional space and then one or more spaces), then we append "Compression delayed".

   But note: the value after Compression might be anything (yes, no, etc.) and we want to set it to delayed.

   However, the rule says "Set Compression to delayed", so we are changing the value.

   We'll do:

      If we find a line that matches: /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+/ 
          then we replace that line with: 
              [the matched part without the trailing spaces?] + "delayed"
          But note: we matched the Compression and the following spaces, so we want to keep the Compression and then put "delayed" (and drop the old value and the extra spaces we matched?).

          Actually, we matched: 
              [optional whitespace] + "Compression" + [one or more spaces]

          We want to end up with:
              [optional whitespace] + "Compression delayed"   [and then the rest of the line?]

          But we don't know what comes after the spaces we matched. 

          To keep it simple and since the problem does not specify comments, we'll assume there are no comments and replace the whole line.

      Given the remediation script in the finding replaces the whole line, we'll do the same.

   Therefore, algorithm:

      Let content = read_file("/etc/ssh/sshd_config")
      Let lines = split content by newline
      Let found = false
      Let new_lines = []
      For each line in lines:
          If the line matches /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+$/ 
              then we push "Compression delayed" to new_lines
              and set found = true
          Else
              push the original line to new_lines
      If not found:
          push "Compression delayed" to new_lines
      Let new_content = join new_lines by newline
      write_file("/etc/ssh/sshd_config", new_content)

   However, note: the pattern /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+$/ 
        matches a line that has (after optional space): 
            Compression, then one or more spaces, and then the end of the line.

        This matches lines like:
            "Compression   "
            "  Compression  "
            "Compression          "

        But not:
            "Compression yes"   -> because after the spaces we have "yes", not end of line.
            "Compression delayed" -> because after the spaces we have "delayed", not end of line.

        So this pattern does not match our target line.

   We want to match lines that have Compression and then any value (which we don't care) and possibly trailing spaces.

   We want to match: 
        /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+[^[:space:]]*/ 

   But note: we don't care about the value after the spaces, so we can match until the end of the line? 

   We can do: /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+.*/ 

   This matches:
        optional space, then Compression, then one or more spaces, then any characters (including none) until end of line.

   Then we replace the whole line with "Compression delayed"

   This will work for:
        "Compression yes" -> becomes "Compression delayed"
        "Compression no"  -> becomes "Compression delayed"
        "Compression delayed" -> becomes "Compression delayed" (so if we find it, we replace it with itself? but we already checked for the exact line and didn't find it? so we skip this step if we found the exact line)

   But wait: we first checked for the exact line "Compression delayed" (with optional whitespace around) and if found we skip.

   So in the loop, we are only processing lines that are not the exact line "Compression delayed" (with optional whitespace).

   Then we look for lines that start with optional space, then Compression, then one or more spaces, and then anything (so we capture the Compression and the spaces and the value) and replace the whole line with "Compression delayed".

   Then if we didn't find any line to change, we append.

   However, note: the exact line we are looking for might have optional whitespace at the beginning and end? 

   We did: 
        If the line matches /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+delayed[[:space:]]*$/ 
          then we found the exact line.

   So let's break down:

      Step 1: Check for exact line (with optional whitespace around) for "Compression delayed"
          pattern: /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+delayed[[:space:]]*$/

      Step 2: If not found in step 1, then for each line:
          if the line matches /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+.*$/ 
              then replace with "Compression delayed"
          else keep the line

      Step 3: If we did not do any replacement in step 2 (i.e., we didn't find any line starting with Compression and then spaces), then append "Compression delayed"

   But note: step 1 might have found a line that is the exact line, so we skip step 2 and 3.

   However, we can combine: we do step 1 and if we found at least one exact line, we are done and skip step 2 and 3.

   Alternatively, we can do:

      Let found_exact = false
      Let found_to_change = false
      Let new_lines = []
      For each line in lines:
          if line matches /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+delayed[[:space:]]*$/:
              push line to new_lines   // we keep the exact line as is
              set found_exact = true
          else if line matches /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+.*$/:
              push "Compression delayed" to new_lines
              set found_to_change = true
          else:
              push line to new_lines
      If not found_exact and not found_to_change:
          push "Compression delayed" to new_lines

   Then write the new_lines.

   But note: if we found an exact line, we don't change it and we don't add a duplicate.

   This meets the requirement: if the exact line is present, we do nothing; if not, we change the first line we find that starts with Compression and then spaces (to set the value to delayed) and if we don't find any such line, we add.

   However, what if there are multiple lines starting with Compression? 
        We change the first one we find and leave the others? 
        But the rule only requires one setting.

   We'll change the first occurrence and leave the rest? 
        But note: having multiple Compression lines is invalid? 
        We are only required to have one.

   To be safe, we change the first occurrence we find and leave the rest? 
        But the problem doesn't specify.

   Given the context of security hardening, we want to ensure that Compression is set to delayed. 
        Having multiple lines might cause the last one to win? 
        But we don't know the behavior.

   We'll follow: if we find an exact line, we do nothing and break (but we continue to check for exact lines? we don't break the loop, we just mark found_exact and keep the line).

   Then if we don't find an exact line, we change the first line we find that matches the pattern (to set to delayed) and then we break? 
        But we don't break the loop because we want to check for exact lines in the rest? 
        Actually, we want to change only one line? 

   However, the remediation script in the finding does not specify changing only one.

   We'll do: 
        We are going to change every line that matches the pattern (to set to delayed) if we haven't found an exact line? 
        But that would change multiple lines.

   Alternatively, we change only the first line we find that matches the pattern (if no exact line) and then we stop changing? 
        But we continue to check for exact lines? 

   Given the complexity and to keep it simple, and since the remediation script in the finding replaces every line that matches the pattern (without checking for exact line first) and then appends if none found, we'll do:

      We are going to:
        - If we find at least one exact line (Compression delayed with optional whitespace), we do nothing to the file (leave it as is) and we are done.
        - Else, we change every line that matches the pattern (Compression followed by one or more spaces and then anything) to "Compression delayed"
        - And if we didn't change any line (meaning we didn't find any line starting with Compression and then spaces), we append "Compression delayed"

   This is what the remediation script in the finding does, except it doesn't check for the exact line first.

   But we are adding the exact line check.

   So we do:

      Let content = read_file("/etc/ssh/sshd_config")
      Let lines = split by newline
      Let found_exact = false
      Let changed = false
      Let new_lines = []
      For each line in lines:
          if line matches /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+delayed[[:space:]]*$/:
              push line to new_lines
              set found_exact = true
          else if line matches /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+.*$/:
              push "Compression delayed" to new_lines
              set changed = true
          else:
              push line to new_lines
      If not found_exact and not changed:
          push "Compression delayed" to new_lines
      Let new_content = join new_lines by newline
      write_file("/etc/ssh/sshd_config", new_content)

   This way:
      - If there is an exact line, we leave it and we don't change any other line (because we don't enter the else-if for the pattern to change) and we don't append (because found_exact is true).
      - If there is no exact line but we found at least one line to change, we change every such line to "Compression delayed" and we don't append.
      - If there is no exact line and no line to change, we append one "Compression delayed".

   However, note: changing every line that matches the pattern might be overkill. 
        But the rule only requires one setting. 
        Having multiple Compression lines is not standard, but we are setting them all to delayed which is consistent.

   We'll go with this.

   Let's test with examples:

   Example 1: 
        Input: 
            Compression delayed
        Steps:
          Line: "Compression delayed" -> matches the exact line pattern -> push as is, found_exact=true.
          Then we don't change any other line (none) and we don't append.
          Output: "Compression delayed"

   Example 2:
        Input:
            Compression yes
        Steps:
          Line: "Compression yes" -> 
              does not match exact line (because after Compression and spaces we have "yes", not "delayed")
              but matches the change pattern: /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+.*$/ 
                  -> we push "Compression delayed", set changed=true
          Then we don't append because changed is true.
          Output: "Compression delayed"

   Example 3:
        Input:
            # Compression yes
            Port 22
        Steps:
          Line1: "# Compression yes" -> 
              does not match exact line (because of the #) 
              does not match change pattern (because of the #) -> push as is
          Line2: "Port 22" -> push as is
          Then we have not found_exact and not changed -> append "Compression delayed"
          Output:
              # Compression yes
              Port 22
              Compression delayed

   Example 4:
        Input:
            Compression   yes
            Compression no
        Steps:
          Line1: "Compression   yes" -> matches change pattern -> push "Compression delayed", changed=true
          Line2: "Compression no" -> matches change pattern -> push "Compression delayed", changed=true (still true)
          Then we don't append.
          Output:
              Compression delayed
              Compression delayed

   This sets both to delayed, which is acceptable.

   Example 5:
        Input:
            (empty)
        Steps:
          We have no lines -> not found_exact and not changed -> append "Compression delayed"
          Output:
              Compression delayed

   This meets the requirement.

   We'll implement accordingly.

   Note: We must use the tools: run_cmd, read_file, write_file.

   We assume:
      read_file(path) -> returns the content of the file as a string
      write_file(path, content) -> writes the content to the file
      We are not given run_cmd? We don't need to run a command, we are only reading and writing files.

   But note: the problem says we have access to run_cmd, read_file, write_file.

   We don't need run_cmd for this task.

   Let's code accordingly.

   However, note: the file might be large? We are reading the whole file, which is acceptable for sshd_config.

   We'll write the code in the form of a script that uses these functions.

   But note: the problem does not specify the language. We assume we can write in a shell-like pseudocode? 
        However, the instruction says: "You MUST use the provided tools: run_cmd, read_file, write_file."

   We'll write:

      content = read_file("/etc/ssh/sshd_config")
      lines = split(content, "\n")
      found_exact = false
      changed = false
      new_lines = []
      for line in lines:
          if line matches /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+delayed[[:space:]]*$/:
              new_lines.append(line)
              found_exact = true
          else if line matches /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+.*$/:
              new_lines.append("Compression delayed")
              changed = true
          else:
              new_lines.append(line)
      if not found_exact and not changed:
          new_lines.append("Compression delayed")
      new_content = join(new_lines, "\n")
      write_file("/etc/ssh/sshd_config", new_content)

      content = read_file("/etc/ssh/sshd_config")
      lines = split(content, "\n")
      found_exact = false
      changed = false
      new_lines = []
      for line in lines:
          if line =~ /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+delayed[[:space:]]*$/:
              new_lines.append(line)
              found_exact = true
          else if line =~ /^[[:space:]]*Compression[[:space:]]+.*$/:
              new_lines.append("Compression delayed")
              changed = true
          else:
              new_lines.append(line)
      if not found_exact and not changed:
          new_lines.append("Compression delayed")
      new_content = join(new_lines, "\n")
      write_file("/etc/ssh/sshd_config", new_content)
